channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: I gave a brand-new AI the memory of all my old chats, here's the free tool I built to do it.
item.description: My AI conversations were scattered across three apps that couldn't remember each other. So I built a private, local-first vault that pulls them together and lets me hand my whole history to any...

item.title: Client Asset Ownership: Should the Agency or the Client Own the Domain and Hosting?
item.description: Article image Client Asset Ownership: Should the Agency or the Client Own the Domain and Hosting? For freelancers and new agency owners, building solid standard operating procedures (SOPs) is one...

item.title: Scaling Codebases Without Platform Bloat
item.description: Redundant compilation lags, bloated CI pipelines and version drift for internal modules quietly drain developer velocity. And normally, it's too late when the leadership gets the whiff of it....

item.title: The Chicken, the Egg, and the Compiler
item.description: Bootstrapping with Bootstrap Hi, I am Dheeraj, and this is my first blog here. So I picked a fitting theme: Bootstrapping with Bootstrap. Bootstrapping is a self-starting process that keeps...

item.title: OpenSearch Serverless NextGen, uv audit, and Why Schema Constraints Break Small Models
item.description: This week's tooling moves cluster around a common theme: eliminating the overhead tax on developer workflows. AWS cut idle costs for search, uv folded security scanning into dependency resolution,...

item.title: Chrome Extension Development Setup: The 2026 Starter Guide | ExtensionBooster
item.description: The Chrome extension landscape has shifted significantly. Here's what's working. Chrome Extension Development Setup: The 2026 Starter Guide | ExtensionBooster Most “build your first Chrome...

item.title: I Evaluated 6 Project Management Tools for My 200-Person Team — Here's What I Actually Picked
item.description: Why I Spent Three Months Testing PM Tools I have been a senior project manager for eight years. My team grew from 15 people to 200 across three time zones, and our tool stack became a Frankenstein...

item.title: Shift-Left Meets AI: Catching Bugs Earlier with Predictive ML Models in Your Dev Pipeline
item.description: The Bug Tax Nobody Talks About A bug caught in production costs roughly 100× more to fix than the same bug caught at the requirements stage — a well-documented finding (NIST, IBM) that underpins...

item.title: Durable Objects + GLM-5.2 IDOR beats Claude
item.description: Three themes dominated AI tooling this week: infrastructure finally catching up to agent runtime requirements, open-weight models closing the gap on specialized security tasks, and the slow death...

item.title: The Most Complex Code Is the One the User Abandons
item.description: A lot of times, the tech industry forgets the golden rule: software isn't the end goal — it's just the means to an end. Recently, while talking to small business owners and watching the daily...

item.title: Citrix Patches Six NetScaler Flaws Allowing File Read and Denial-of-Service
item.description: Citrix on Tuesday released security updates to address multiple flaws in NetScaler ADC (formerly Citrix ADC) and NetScaler Gateway (formerly Citrix Gateway) that could be exploited by an attacker...

item.title: The “Father of the Internet” is finally retiring
item.description: Vinton Cerf, one of the creators of the protocols underlying the internet, will step down as Google's chief internet evangelist next week.

item.title: Trump drops restrictions on Anthropic’s Mythos and Fable models
item.description: Anthropic said it would begin restoring access to the Fable on July 1.

item.title: Wayve launches $85M employee tender offer at $8.5B valuation
item.description: Wayve’s offering is part of a growing trend of AI startups using employee tenders as a strategic tool to attract and retain talent.

item.title: Meta is adding ridiculous ‘rate limits’ and a soft paywall to its smart glasses
item.description: Would you pay $20 a month for access to AI hardware you already own? That appears to be one of Meta's next bets. This week, it quietly announced that your glasses' Conversation Focus feature will...

item.title: Anthropic’s long-sidelined Fable 5 is greenlit to return
item.description: After weeks of negotiating with the Trump administration, Anthropic is finally going to be able to bring Claude Fable 5 back online. In a post on X, Anthropic said it plans to begin restoring...

item.title: The Trump Administration Is Lifting Its Export Controls on Anthropic’s Mythos and Fable AI Models
item.description: The White House is easing restrictions on Anthropic’s most advanced AI models weeks after ordering the company to suspend access for foreign nationals.

item.title: Dish files for bankruptcy, but not shutting down
item.description: Dish, the company that operates Dish TV and Sling TV, has filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy," as reported earlier by Reuters. The plan will allow the EchoStar-owned company to continue to wind down...

item.title: OpenClaw is finally available on Android and iOS
item.description: The free open source agentic program is finally invading your phone.

item.title: Claude Science is Anthropic’s newest flagship product
item.description: At an event for pharmaceutical executives, biotech founders, and researchers on Tuesday, Anthropic announced Claude Science, a major new product intended to support scientific research in the same...

item.title: Amazon fined $2.25 million for failing to help identity theft victims
item.description: The Federal Trade Commission fined Amazon $2.25 million to settle claims that the company failed to help customers who fell victim to identity theft, as reported earlier by Bloomberg. In its...

item.title: The DeepMind trio who built a poker AI are now making money for quant hedge funds
item.description: EquiLibre Technologies, a Prague-based AI lab founded by three ex-DeepMind researchers, is now valued at more than $500 million.

item.title: New attack provides one more reason why AI browsers are a bad idea
item.description: Telling an LLM that 2 + 2 = 5 is enough to make it follow forbidden instructions.

item.title: Acer’s Swift Go 16 is a lot of laptop for $900
item.description: As high memory and storage prices have driven up the cost of everything from consoles to computers, finding a competent laptop for under $1,000 has become a challenge. Thankfully, the Acer Swift...

item.title: Google’s NotebookLM can sum up your research in a TikTok-style clip
item.description: Google's NotebookLM is adding a new way to catch up on your notes: TikTok-style AI videos. The new feature is rolling out to Google AI Ultra and Pro subscribers, allowing NotebookLM to generate...

item.title: Google introduces a faster, cheaper image generator with Nano Banana 2 Lite
item.description: Google is updating its image generator to make it faster and cheaper, making it a more useful tool for creators looking to make AI content.

item.title: LG’s 27-inch Tandem OLED gaming monitor is cheaper than ever
item.description: The price of LG’s UltraGear 27GX700A-B 1440p gaming monitor that came out last August has dropped to nearly its best price yet. You can grab it at Amazon for $484.99 or at LG for $499.99...

item.title: ScarfBench: Benchmarking AI Agents for Enterprise Java Framework Migration
007 First Light’s developer lays off staff but claims its next franchise will continue
item.description: IO Interactive, the studio behind the Hitman series and 007 First Light, announced that it is laying off staff after a relationship with an "external partner" on its next big franchise, Project...

item.title: Nvidia competitor Etched hits $5B valuation, $1B in sales for AI chip
item.description: Nvidia AI chip competitor Etched says it has already booked $1 billion under contract for the inference systems powered by its chip.
